old_claim.note: Cloud Data Warehouses provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because relational data warehousing predates managed cloud data warehouses; cloud warehouses are a later deployment category, not a prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: AWS announcement for Amazon Redshift as a cloud data warehouse service in 2012.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: AWS announced Amazon Redshift as a managed cloud data warehouse service in 2012.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A 2012 managed cloud warehouse cannot be a prerequisite for relational data warehousing scoped to 1980s analytical relational systems; the relationship is chronological successor or implementation variant.

ontology_before: The graph made earlier relational data warehousing depend on a later cloud service category.
ontology_after: Relational data warehousing depends on relational DBMS and enterprise data needs; cloud warehouses are later managed-cloud implementations.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-10-random-sample-quality-fixes keeps this edge and other stale cloud-warehouse prerequisite edges absent.
would_reject_if: The relational_data_warehousing node were narrowed to cloud-native relational data warehousing only.
A source showed relational data warehousing did not exist before managed cloud warehouses.
The graph split cloud_data_warehouses into a broader non-cloud data-warehouse node and rewired dependencies accordingly.
short_reason: Relational data warehousing predates managed cloud data warehouses.
